name: Lakeside Transportation
principals: Patrick Veal (local owner/operator); John Benish Jr. (Cook-Illinois CEO, family owners)
districts_or_customers: Lake County, IL school districts (Waukegan, Mundelein, Libertyville area); large special education contract anchored launch
recent_news: Lakeside is a subsidiary brand of Cook-Illinois Corporation, the largest family-owned school bus company in the U.S.; began Aug 2004 with a large SPED contract and doubled in size the next year. Cook-Illinois has acquired and consolidated multiple IL operators over time.
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in target — Lakeside is already a subsidiary of Cook-Illinois Corp, the largest family-owned school bus platform in the U.S. Any approach would be a platform-level conversation with Cook-Illinois (Benish family), not a Lakeside-specific tuck-in. The Addison, IL address in the source data likely reflects a Cook-Illinois corporate/registered address, not a separate yard.

name: Easy Ride Express Bus
principals: Nassir Abdi (listed as principal); Abdinasir Hirsi (team member named on website)
districts_or_customers: Weekly intercity passenger service Columbus OH <-> Minneapolis MN, with additional service options to Chicago, Indianapolis, Dayton, Milwaukee. Office at Banadir Mall, 3246 Cleveland Ave, Columbus OH 43224.
recent_news: No M&A or news identified. Website markets weekly Friday-evening Columbus departures and Sunday-morning Minneapolis returns; serves Somali diaspora community.
fit_notes: NOT a fit for Summit. This is an intercity/charter passenger bus operator (Columbus-Minneapolis Somali community line), not a school-bus or student-transport contractor. The 'school=1' flag in the source dataset appears to be a misclassification. Exclude from tuck-in pipeline.

name: BME Transit LLC
districts_or_customers: Penn Hills / Pittsburgh PA metro area; not listed on the official Pittsburgh Public Schools approved-carrier roster, suggesting smaller charter/sub-contract or non-PPS district work
recent_news: BBB lists BME Transit LLC, Pittsburgh PA, in the transportation category. PA business records show LLC formation in 2011 (the source dataset's 'year=1970' appears erroneous). Penn Hills SD's primary contractor is Krise Transportation, and the district has been shifting some middle/high routes to Pittsburgh Regional Transit to cut costs.
fit_notes: Very small (~7 employees, sub-$0.5M revenue est.) Pittsburgh-area operator. Possibly a real independent tuck-in candidate but extremely sub-scale and without confirmed school-district contracts — would likely be a route/asset purchase rather than a platform. Validate fleet count and contract base before pursuing.

name: Coast Mountain Bus Co
owner_or_parent: South Coast British Columbia Transportation Authority (TransLink) — IF this is the real Coast Mountain Bus Company; otherwise data record appears to be a mislabeled/duplicate entry
service_types: public transit (conventional bus, community shuttle, trolley)
districts_or_customers: Metro Vancouver, British Columbia (TransLink network) — NOT a California school district contractor
recent_news: Coast Mountain Bus Company is the wholly-owned bus-operations subsidiary of TransLink in Metro Vancouver BC. No California school-bus operation under this name was verifiable; the La Puente CA address (13600 Alcade St) and 482 employees almost certainly reflect a source-data mismatch or a stale/incorrect record.
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in target. Either (a) this is the Vancouver BC TransLink-owned public transit operator — Canadian, government-owned, not for sale and not a school-bus contractor — or (b) the dataset has conflated a small unrelated La Puente CA entity with the BC company's branding. Recommend purging this record from the pipeline pending data hygiene; do not invest research time.

name: Care Transport
districts_or_customers: Rural southeast Georgia (Brantley County area around Hoboken/Waycross); likely Medicaid NEMT and possibly some SPED/social-services routes given service mix flag
recent_news: No M&A or news found. Note: caretransport.us is a different Care Transport Inc. based in Ann Arbor MI (est. 1989) — unrelated to the Hoboken GA entity. Georgia Medicaid NEMT is brokered statewide by Verida (effective April 2026), which is restructuring the GA NEMT subcontractor landscape.
website: caretransport.us (NOTE: this URL belongs to the unrelated Michigan company; the GA entity may not have a website)
fit_notes: Very small rural-GA operator (~7 employees, ~$0.9M est. revenue), 1988 vintage with an aging owner — succession signal is high in principle, but the business mix appears to be NEMT/social-services rather than school-bus contracting. Likely too small and off-thesis for a Summit tuck-in; revisit only if confirmed to hold real SPED/student-transport contracts.
